source: MLB.com (Aug 2020), SABR Bio Project, NLBM Barrier Breakers
methodology: Integration dates verified against SABR BioProject entries, Baseball Reference game logs, and MLB.com official list (Aug 2020). Tryout dates verified against Wendell Smith Papers (Baseball Hall of Fame), Pittsburgh Courier, and Chicago Defender contemporaneous reporting. Where the first Black player categorization involves a player of Afro-Caribbean or Afro-Latin descent, the categorization follows MLB's official recognition and is labeled at the point of claim. SABR's Jackie 75 Pioneers list uses a broader definition that includes Carlos Bernier (Pirates, Apr 22, 1953) and Chico Fernandez (Phillies, Apr 16, 1957); this dataset uses MLB's official designations of Curt Roberts and John Kennedy respectively.

data.tryoutContext: Joe Bostic, sports editor of the People's Voice, brought two Negro League veterans to the Dodgers' spring training camp at West Point. Branch Rickey conducted the tryout but did not sign either player.
Muchnick pressed the Braves for a tryout in April 1945, but the club declined after the Red Sox held theirs. No Braves tryout was ever held.
Manager Jimmy Dykes praised Robinson ('He's worth $50,000 of anybody's money') but the team never contacted them again. The tryout was arranged through political and press pressure.

data.tryoutPlayers: Roy Campanella, Sammy Hughes, David Barnhill
data.tryoutContext: Owner William Benswanger invited three Negro League players after sustained pressure from the Pittsburgh Courier and the Daily Worker. Campanella noted the invitation letter 'contained so many buts that I was discouraged.' Benswanger canceled before the tryout was held.
data.source: SABR BioProject, MLB.com, Pittsburgh Courier, Bucs Dugout
data.tryoutOutcome: Canceled. No tryout held. No signings.

data.tryoutContext: SABR's Pioneers list credits Chico Fernandez (Apr 16, 1957) as the Phillies' first player of color, but MLB and the NLBM recognize John Kennedy (Apr 22, 1957) as the first African American Phillie. Fernandez was Afro-Cuban.
data.source: SABR BioProject, SABR Game Project, MLB.com, NLBM Barrier Breakers

data.tryoutPlayers: Jackie Robinson, Sam Jethroe, Marvin Williams
data.tryoutContext: Boston city councilman Isadore Muchnick threatened to revoke the Red Sox' Sunday game permit. Wendell Smith organized three Negro League players for a 90-minute workout. No follow-up from Red Sox management. Eddie Collins wrote to Smith on April 27, 1945, citing Joe Cronin's broken leg and 'concerns over Negro league contracts.' The Red Sox did not sign a Black player for fourteen more years. Pumpsie Green's signing in 1959 was forced by a Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination investigation.
data.source: SABR BioProject, MLB.com, Wendell Smith Papers (Baseball HOF), Pittsburgh Courier, Boston Globe, Glenn Stout 'Tryout and Fallout'
data.tryoutOutcome: 90-minute workout. No signings. Red Sox became the last team to integrate, 14 years later.

tryoutDatabase.outcome: Tryout held at spring training camp

tryoutDatabase.players: Jackie Robinson, Sam Jethroe, Marvin Williams
tryoutDatabase.source: Wendell Smith Papers (Baseball HOF), Pittsburgh Courier, Boston Globe, SABR BioProject
